title: coleus ingratus
intro: Coleus ingratus, also known as the Painted Nettle, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Coleus ingratus include its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
